authoritative_sources.title: Occupational Outlook Handbook: Lodging Managers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is a U.S. federal statistical agency, and its lodging-manager profile provides a public baseline for hotel operations duties including guest-room inspection, operations coordination, staffing, and guest complaint handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: day-of-stay operating intake, room readiness, staff coordination, guest-impact execution, and completion review

authoritative_sources.title: 11-9081.00 - Lodging Managers
authoritative_sources.publisher: O*NET Resource Center / U.S. Department of Labor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET is sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor and provides task-level lodging-manager duties that support workflow claims about room inspection, employee assignment, guest-service coordination, and hotel policy execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: room inspection, staffing assignment, operational coordination, complaint triage, and shift execution

authoritative_sources.title: Hotel Industry Launches Training Course to Advance Safe Stay Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHLA is the leading U.S. hotel industry association, and its Safe Stay material is a recognized hotel-industry operating baseline for cleanliness, health, and safety training that constrains room readiness, housekeeping, and guest-safety execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: room turn, cleaning and safety readiness, housekeeping training, and health-sensitive operating execution

authoritative_sources.title: Hotel 2.0 Availability and Reservation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OpenTravel Alliance is the recognized travel-technology standards body; its hotel availability and reservation model anchors vendor-neutral hotel terminology for availability, room type, rate plan, reservation, and booking records used by PMS, CRS, and channel workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: booking, availability, reservation-state, rate-plan, and PMS or CRS mismatch review
used_for: PMS, CRS, and channel mismatch handling

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official short-term-lodging guidance defines total-price disclosure, mandatory-fee treatment, and truthful fee descriptions that limit operational responses to amenity, resort-fee, and outage complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rate, fee, booking, amenity outage, and service-recovery boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance and is authoritative for merchant-versus-issuer boundaries relevant to duplicate charges, incidental holds, refund-not-posted complaints, and folio disputes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: folio mismatch, duplicate incidental hold, refund status, and issuer-boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal civil-rights authority for ADA Title III enforcement, and the lodging reservations section directly governs accessible-room descriptions, holding, blocking, and reservation-system handling that hotel operations must not improvise around.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible-room status review, room-readiness blocker handling, and front-office handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Travel-associated Outbreaks as a Public Health Issue
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the primary U.S. public-health authority, and this accommodation-specific outbreak guidance directly addresses guest-list requests, incoming or past guest notification, and escalation behavior after suspected exposure events at accommodations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: outbreak-sensitive incident handling, guest-list evidence, and safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Securing Public Gatherings
authoritative_sources.publisher: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency / DHS TRIPwire
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the U.S. infrastructure-security authority, and its public-gatherings guidance provides authoritative preparedness, risk mitigation, staff training, local responder coordination, and reporting expectations for accessible venues such as hotel lobbies, meeting areas, restaurants, and event spaces.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security-sensitive incident intake, staff and guest safety escalation, and property-leadership coordination

authoritative_sources.title: Securing Property Management Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is the U.S. federal standards body for cybersecurity; SP 1800-27 is tailored to hospitality PMS environments that store guest personal information and payment data, making it authoritative for PMS evidence, privacy, and cyber-risk boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PMS dependency review, privacy-sensitive operating evidence, and cyber incident routing
used_for: guest data and payment data handling caveats
system outage or suspicious PMS behavior escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Are call center environments considered sensitive areas for PCI DSS Requirement 9.1.1?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and this FAQ directly constrains how hotel operations and guest-service workflows handle systems that process, transmit, or store cardholder data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-data handling, incidental-hold support, service-ticket evidence, and card-data stop conditions

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect room access, reservation changes, refund reroutes, and guest-profile updates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch handling, suspicious account-use escalation, and loss-prevention routing
used_for: refund reroute and guest-profile update risk handling

workflow_stages.name: Operational intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm property, date, shift, request class, guest-impact posture, records of truth, and whether the work is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before acting.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Occupancy, room-status, and workload evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Validate PMS occupancy, arrivals, departures, stayovers, room assignments, room status, housekeeping board, inspection notes, work orders, staffing roster, and service tickets before moving work.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle-state execution
workflow_stages.description: Move accepted hotel work through ready, in progress, blocked, hold, rework, escalated, pending verification, or complete only when evidence supports the transition.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Operational exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Hold, rework, or escalate rooms, staffing, service tickets, outages, booking mismatches, and guest-impact blockers instead of silently skipping required process steps.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Pricing, booking, payment, and accessibility boundary review
workflow_stages.description: When operational execution touches rate plans, fees, refunds, incidental holds, room control, accessible rooms, or booking mismatches, summarize the facts and route to the correct owner without approving exceptions.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, fraud, PMS-security, and loss-prevention stop handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop normal execution and escalate when public-health, physical security, cyber, payment-card, identity, or loss-prevention signals change whether the lane can safely proceed.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and shift handoff capture
workflow_stages.description: Close only when PMS, housekeeping, work-order, staffing, service-ticket, incident, outage, and approval evidence supports completion; otherwise retain reversible state and hand off with blockers.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: PMS occupancy boards, arrivals, departures, stayovers, no-shows, walk lists, room assignments, room inventory states, and folio references
room-status reports, housekeeping boards, room-turn queues, inspection notes, DND notes, out-of-order and out-of-service logs, and clean, dirty, vacant clean, or inspected status
work orders, engineering tickets, maintenance closeout notes, outage logs, elevator, HVAC, water, power, internet, key-system, or POS disruption records
staffing rosters, callout logs, shift handoff notes, labor or union constraints, cross-training limits, and coverage escalation notes
service tickets, guest-impact notes, complaint logs, approved service-recovery records, lost-and-found references, and disruption communications
incident reports, safety or security logs, public-health guest lists, fraud or loss-prevention notes, PMS cyber-risk notes, payment-data handling references, and escalation records
completion checklists, timestamps, accountable owners, approval evidence, open-blocker registers, and audit notes

allowed: Classify hotel operations work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, guest-impacting, safety-sensitive, policy-exception-sensitive, or owned by another lane.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, escalate, or complete operational work when documented execution state and evidence support the move.
Prepare operations status updates, exception summaries, completion evidence records, and handoff packages without overstating authority.
Triage high-volume day-of-stay queues by occupancy, readiness, guest impact, service-level risk, and owner.
escalate: Required PMS, CRS, housekeeping, work-order, staffing, ticketing, incident, payment, safety, fraud, or approval records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires room-control, pricing override, refund exception approval, compensation approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, safety signoff, engineering clearance, or executive approval.
A public-health, physical security, identity mismatch, PMS cyber-risk, or payment-card handling signal changes whether direct execution can proceed.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, brand, supplier, processor, labor, jurisdiction, or property policy materially conflict.
Guest-impact severity or repeat failure makes routine execution unsafe without human review.
refuse: Mark rooms, tickets, outages, staffing fixes, incidents, or service work complete without authoritative closure evidence.
Override pricing, refund, compensation, accessible-room, safety, fraud, room-control, payment, or policy controls without explicit authority.
Ask for or restate full payment-card data in insecure communication channels.
Declare safety, public-health, security, engineering, fraud, or incident closure without the designated owner and evidence.

domain_failure_modes: the lane releases rooms or marks room turns complete when PMS, housekeeping, inspection, or work-order evidence is missing or contradictory
an operational exception is treated as routine execution even though pricing, refund, compensation, walk strategy, or revenue displacement authority is required
a room assignment, accessible-room substitution, key-control, identity, or payment guarantee issue is handled outside front-office authority
a booking or payment mismatch across PMS, CRS, folio, and issuer evidence is forced into an unsupported operational correction
high-volume arrival or service queues skip evidence checks and hide safety, fraud, guest-harm, or policy-exception signals
outbreak, safety, PMS-security, card-data, or fraud indicators are ignored in the name of speed or guest appeasement
tenant staffing, labor, brand, or room-readiness rules are guessed instead of retrieved
the industry overlay collapses into generic task tracking instead of hotel occupancy, room-status, staffing, work-order, service-ticket, outage, and completion terminology

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific lodging fee rules, local public-health instructions, labor or union rules, and property-specific safety plans can materially narrow operational handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, property policies, labor constraints, public-health direction, emergency plans, and brand standards before applying property-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Hotel PMS, housekeeping, work-order, and staffing systems use tenant-specific status names and may disagree on source-of-truth precedence.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane records system conflicts and routes to the designated owner instead of guessing. No blocking ambiguity remains because the escalation and hold behavior is explicit.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public sources describe hotel duties and constraints but do not define tenant-specific staffing ratios, room-release SLAs, service-recovery thresholds, or final approval matrices.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Those details are tenant-required facts. The validated package stays safe by requiring retrieval and refusing unsupported execution changes.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, systems of record, pricing and booking boundaries, completion evidence, safety, fraud, payment-security, and failure behavior.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdictional, property, brand, labor, processor, supplier, emergency, and system-of-record differences are handled through runtime retrieval, hold states, and escalation rules.

The lane stops at hotel operations execution, exception packaging, and evidence-backed state movement. Pricing, refunds, discretionary compensation, front-office room control, fraud findings, engineering clearance, and safety signoff remain outside the lane.
